summary.intent: Address six Codex review threads on PR #683 (Editable component) and establish a rule that mutation tests must not be rerun after a PR is opened.
summary.outcome: All six Codex review threads were fixed in code and spec, snapshots accepted, CI checks passed, and the no-rerun-mutation rule was codified in AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/implementation/README.md, and the post-implementation-audit skill. Changes were committed and pushed.

repo: Mutation tests are a one-time delivery gate for new/materially-changed agnostic components, not a per-review-round requirement. After PR creation, agents must NOT rerun cargo mutants during Codex review rounds unless explicitly asked.
The Codex review loop signals: 👀 = reviewing, +1 = approved, unresolved threads without 👀 = needs action. The loop polls every 60 seconds.
Snapshot updates from attribute/behavior changes (required, tabindex, trigger) are expected and accepted via `cargo insta accept` then re-run.
The post-implementation-audit skill (Phase 3) was the authoritative location for the mutation gate rule; waiting-for-codex-review skill did not explicitly require mutation retesting.
The review-fix validation suite is: fmt, clippy, spec validate (cargo xtask spec validate), full insta snapshot validation, and focused coverage — no mutation.

code.finding: Tab key behavior follows effective blur-submit, ActivateMode::None blocks preview Enter, disabled preview leaves tab order, required sets native required attribute, and triggers stay keyboard-reachable.
Spec was updated to document Tab/blur-submit behavior, ActivateMode::None Enter blocking, disabled preview tab order, required attribute, and keyboard-reachable triggers.
Added explicit prohibition: after PR is opened, agents MUST NOT rerun mutation tests during Codex review rounds.

workflow: apply_patch context mismatches require narrowing to exact surrounding text; rg -n on the target file first to find precise line ranges.
Staging files explicitly by name (git add <file1> <file2> ...) rather than git add -A is the established pattern here.
pkill -f '<cargo command> || true' is the safe pattern to abort in-flight cargo mutants runs.
cargo insta accept followed by re-run is the correct cycle when snapshot changes are intentional.

friction: apply_patch failed on first attempt due to context mismatch; required a second lookup with rg -n to find exact surrounding lines before patching.
Mutation tests were started for the review-fix round before the user intervened — agent inferred mutation was required per-round when the docs did not mandate it.
The mutation run had to be killed mid-flight (pkill), wasting several minutes of CI/local compute.
CLAUDE.md did not show a diff after patching, suggesting the patch may not have applied cleanly there (git diff showed it unstaged).
open_items: Verify CLAUDE.md actually received the no-rerun-mutation rule (git diff showed it was not staged alongside the other files in the final commit).
Codex re-trigger and thread resolution not shown in transcript — confirm threads were replied to and resolved and Codex re-reviewed.
CI formatting check was still in progress when the transcript ends — confirm it went green before merge.
